California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
llarch 27, l-99O
」ohn Santoya
4■55 E. Raco
Fresno, CA 93702
Dear llr. Santoya:
auESltIeNS
(1) when does a group of citizens who are circulating
petitions to qualify a Local ballot neasure become a committee
under the Act?
(2) what disclosure requirements apply to this type of
committee?
CONCLUSION
(1) The citizens group becomes a coramittee $rhen 91,000 or
more in contributions have been received to quali.fy an initiative,
referendum or recall measure.
(2) Ballot neasure qualification connittees nust file a
statement of organization (Form 410) within 10 days of receiving
91,000 or more. A campaign disclosure statement (Form 450 or
420) nust be filed not later than 21 days after any petitions are
filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions,
whichever is earlier.
L/ 
covernnent Code Sections 81OOO-91015. AIf statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. A1l references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
Re:  Your Request For Adv■
ce
Our File No. A-90-■
59
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Politicat Reform Act.l/
428 J StrCCt,Suitc 800 ● P.().B(、、807 0 Sticranlct、to CA 958o■‐0ド07 ● (916)322´ ヽ(、60


John Santoya
Page Two
FACTS
A group of Fresno citizens circulated petitions against the
use of water meters. In your letter you indicated that the
petitions would have to be submitted to the city council and that
the city council lrould set a date for an election. 
However, lre
vrere advised by Becky Klisch, Fresno City Clerk, that the neasure
is an initiative 
which will qualify for the ballot on the basis of
signature gathering a1one.
You advi.sed ne that the citizens group had received g1,OOO or
more to qualify the measure. As we discussed on February 27,
1990, your group qualified as a committee and was required to file
a statement of organization (Forrn 410) s/ithin 1,0 days of receiving
$1, o00.
ANALYSIS
The term rrrneasuretr includes:
...any proposition which is subrnitted or is intended to
be subrnitted to a popular vote at an election by initiative,
referendum or recall procedure whether or not it qualifies
for the ballot. 
(Section 82043.)
Therefore, an initiative, 
referendum or recaLL becomes a
measure when the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions
to gualify the measure for the baIIot. 
(fn re Fontana (L976) z
FPPC Opinions 25, copy enclosed.)
_ A recipient committee is any person, organization or entity
which receives contributions totaling $1,ooo or more in a calendar
year. (Section 82013.) A statenent of organization is required
to be filed with the Secretary of State within 10 days of
qualifying as a recipient conmittee. your cornmittee must also
file a copy of the statement with the Fresno city clerk. 
(Section
84101. )
A canpaign disclosure statement (Forn 420 or Forrn 450, copies
enclosed) must be filed not later than 21 days after any petitions
are fiIed, .or 2l days after the deadline for filing petition=,
whichever is earrier. 
(section 84200.5(f).) 
This 
- statenent nust
be filed with the Fresno city clerk. 
rf th; measure qualifies for
the balIot, and the committee receives contributio.,= or ^ii".
expenditures -to support passage of the measure, it wiff Ue
required to- file pre-election statements during ttre six-rnontir
period in which the measure is being voted upoi. In addition, the
committee wilr be required to file ierni-annull statenrents which
are due on July 3l.st and January 31st each year until the
committee terrninates. (see pagl 23 of the -rnfornation llanuar. on
campaign Discrosure provisions of the poriticar n"r"i^ -a"I, 
""pv
enclosed. )


John Santoya
Page Three
If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact ne at (915) 322-5662.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
